mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, supporting equity multiples and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month when the easing move is broad and sustained.
failure_condition: Reject this hypothesis if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months when FEDFUNDS trends down for at least 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level above neutral tightens financing conditions and suppresses equity valuation multiples, so SPX returns over the next 20 trading days are negative while policy remains restrictive.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with FEDFUNDS above 5.0% do not show negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate of negative returns falls below 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates weakening household income and demand, which should precede lower S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth above trend signals resilient labor income and consumption, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive SPX returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases for 3 consecutive monthly prints
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive more often than not following three-month payroll acceleration, or if the signal becomes negative after accounting for revisions over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad consumer and housing demand, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and generate positive SPX returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-HOUST strength signal does not outperform either series alone on 60-day SPX returns, or if conditional returns are not positive in at least 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO alongside stable or falling CPIAUCSL implies improving supply-side output without inflation pressure, which supports equity multiples and positive SPX returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 20-day SPX returns do not concentrate in periods where INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL is flat or declining, or if the interaction term loses significance in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: If GDPC1 accelerates while PAYEMS also grows, real activity and income momentum should reinforce earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth accelerates and PAYEMS is above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDP-payroll expansion regime fails to deliver positive 60-day SPX returns more often than 55%, or if the effect disappears after revisions to GDPC1.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a simultaneous rise in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS indicates inflation pressure is forcing policy restraint, which should be bearish for SPX over the next 5 trading days through higher discount rates and multiple compression.
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not negative during months when inflation and policy rates both rise, or if the interaction signal is positive in more than 40% of tightening episodes.
